raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "affirmative assertion of human agency and self-determination, expressed poetically to inspire reflection on personal responsibility for identity",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "individuals possess meaningful capacity to shape their own character or life trajectory",
    "identity is at least partly the product of one's own actions rather than wholly external or predetermined",
    "self-creation is an ongoing or iterative process"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent as evocative metaphor; the apparent circularity is resolved by distinguishing the initial self from the self that is continually fashioned",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "context values empowerment, moral responsibility, or psychological growth and treats the claim as inspirational rather than descriptive of literal ontology",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "audience interprets it as denying all external, biological, or structural influences on identity, or applies it to justify blaming individuals for circumstances they cannot realistically control"
}
